    21→class CircuitError(Exception):
    22→    """Base error for circuit-related errors."""

   107→# =============================================================================
   108→# AST Nodes
   109→# =============================================================================
   110→
   111→class ASTNode:
   112→    pass
   113→
   114→
   115→class SignalRef(ASTNode):
   116→    """Reference to a signal with optional slice/index.
   117→
   118→    name: signal name
   119→    msb: None for scalar reference, otherwise MSB of slice/index
   120→    lsb: None for scalar reference, LSB of slice (for single bit, msb=lsb)
   121→    """
   122→    def __init__(self, name: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   123→        self.name = name
   124→        self.msb = msb
   125→        self.lsb = lsb
   126→
   127→    def __repr__(self):
   128→        if self.msb is None:
   129→            return f"SignalRef({self.name})"
   130→        elif self.msb == self.lsb:
   131→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}])"
   132→        else:
   133→            return f"SignalRef({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}])"
   134→
   135→
   136→class Literal(ASTNode):
   137→    """Literal value with width.
   138→
   139→    value: integer value
   140→    width: bit width
   141→    """
   142→    def __init__(self, value: int, width: int):
   143→        self.value = value
   144→        self.width = width
   145→
   146→    def __repr__(self):
   147→        return f"Literal(0b{self.value:0{self.width}b}, width={self.width})"
   148→
   149→
   150→class Concat(ASTNode):
   151→    """Concatenation: {e1, e2, ..., ek}"""
   152→    def __init__(self, parts: List[ASTNode]):
   153→        self.parts = parts
   154→
   155→    def __repr__(self):
   156→        return f"Concat([{', '.join(repr(p) for p in self.parts)}])"
   157→
   158→
   159→class OpCall(ASTNode):
   160→    """Operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   161→    def __init__(self, op: str, args: List[ASTNode]):
   162→        self.op = op
   163→        self.args = args
   164→
   165→    def __repr__(self):
   166→        return f"OpCall({self.op}, [{', '.join(repr(a) for a in self.args)}])"
   167→
   168→
   169→# =============================================================================
   170→# Signal Declaration
   171→# =============================================================================
   172→
   173→class SignalDecl:
   174→    """Signal declaration with width information.
   175→
   176→    name: signal name
   177→    sig_type: 'input', 'output', or 'wire'
   178→    msb: MSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   179→    lsb: LSB of the signal (None for scalar in old format)
   180→    """
   181→    def __init__(self, name: str, sig_type: str, msb: Optional[int] = None, lsb: Optional[int] = None):
   182→        self.name = name
   183→        self.sig_type = sig_type
   184→        self.msb = msb
   185→        self.lsb = lsb
   186→
   187→    @property
   188→    def width(self) -> int:
   189→        if self.msb is None:
   190→            return 1
   191→        return self.msb - self.lsb + 1
   192→
   193→    def __repr__(self):
   194→        if self.msb is None:
   195→            return f"SignalDecl({self.name}, {self.sig_type})"
   196→        return f"SignalDecl({self.name}[{self.msb}:{self.lsb}], {self.sig_type})"
   197→
   198→
   199→# =============================================================================
   200→# Token Types
   201→# =============================================================================
   202→
   203→TOKEN_ID = 'ID'
   204→TOKEN_KW = 'KW'
   205→TOKEN_OP = 'OP'
   206→TOKEN_LITERAL = 'LITERAL'
   207→TOKEN_NUMBER = 'NUMBER'
   208→TOKEN_LBRACE = '{'
   209→TOKEN_RBRACE = '}'
   210→TOKEN_LBRACK = '['
   211→TOKEN_RBRACK = ']'
   212→TOKEN_COLON = ':'
   213→TOKEN_EQ = '='
   214→TOKEN_COMMA = ','
   215→TOKEN_LPAREN = '('
   216→TOKEN_RPAREN = ')'
   217→TOKEN_EOF = 'EOF'
   218→
   219→
   220→class Token:
   221→    def __init__(self, type: str, value: str, line: int, col: int):
   222→        self.type = type
   223→        self.value = value
   224→        self.line = line
   225→        self.col = col
   226→
   227→    def __repr__(self):
   228→        return f"Token({self.type}, {self.value!r}, line={self.line}, col={self.col})"
   229→
   230→
   231→# =============================================================================
   232→# Lexer
   233→# =============================================================================
   234→
   235→def tokenize(line: str, line_num: int) -> List[Token]:
   236→    """Tokenize a single line.
   237→
   238→    Returns list of Token objects.
   239→    """
   240→    tokens = []
   241→    pos = 0
   242→    length = len(line)
   243→
   244→    # Operator patterns (case-insensitive)
   245→    operators = {'AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR', 'NOT', 'BUF',
   246→                 'MUX', 'ITE', 'REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR', 'EQ'}
   247→
   248→    while pos < length:
   249→        col_pos = pos + 1  # 1-based column
   250→
   251→        # Skip whitespace
   252→        if line[pos] in ' \t':
   253→            pos += 1
   254→            continue
   255→
   256→        # Comment
   257→        if line[pos] == '#':
   258→            break  # Rest of line is comment, ignore
   259→
   260→        # Identifiers (letters, underscores, digits after first char)
   261→        if re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_]', line[pos:]):
   262→            match = re.match(r'[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*', line[pos:])
   263→            if match:
   264→                token = match.group()
   265→                upper_token = token.upper()
   266→                if upper_token in operators:
   267→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_OP, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   268→                elif upper_token in {'INPUT', 'OUTPUT', 'WIRE'}:
   269→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_KW, upper_token, line_num, col_pos))
   270→                else:
   271→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_ID, token, line_num, col_pos))
   272→                pos += len(token)
   273→                continue
   274→
   275→        # Numbers (decimal integers for msb:lsb notation)
   276→        if re.match(r'[0-9]', line[pos:]):
   277→            match = re.match(r'[0-9]+', line[pos:])
   278→            if match:
   279→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_NUMBER, match.group(), line_num, col_pos))
   280→                pos += len(match.group())
   281→                continue
   282→
   283→        # Literals: 0, 1 (scalar), sized/unsized vectors
   284→        if line[pos] in '01':
   285→            # Check if this is a sized literal like 8'b1010
   286→            rest = line[pos:]
   287→            sized_match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', rest)
   288→            if sized_match:
   289→                # This is a sized literal
   290→                width = int(sized_match.group(1))
   291→                radix_str = sized_match.group(2).lower()
   292→                value_str = sized_match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   293→
   294→                # Parse based on radix
   295→                if radix_str in ('b', 'h', 'd'):
   296→                    try:
   297→                        if radix_str == 'b':
   298→                            value = int(value_str, 2)
   299→                        elif radix_str == 'h':
   300→                            value = int(value_str, 16)
   301→                        else:
   302→                            value = int(value_str, 10)
   303→                    except ValueError:
   304→                        raise CircParseError(
   305→                            f"Invalid literal: {sized_match.group(0)}",
   306→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   307→                        )
   308→
   309→                    # Check value fits in width
   310→                    if value >= (1 << width):
   311→                        raise CircParseError(
   312→                            f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
   313→                            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   314→                        )
   315→
   316→                    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{width}'{radix_str}{value_str}", line_num, col_pos))
   317→                    pos += len(sized_match.group(0))
   318→                    continue
   319→            else:
   320→                # Scalar literal
   321→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   322→                pos += 1
   323→                continue
   324→
   325→        # Check for 0b or 0x binary/hex literals
   326→        if line[pos:pos+2] in ('0b', '0x'):
   327→            prefix = line[pos:pos+2]
   328→            rest = line[pos+2:]
   329→            # Must have at least one digit after prefix
   330→            match = re.match(r'[0-9a-fA-F]+', rest)
   331→            if match:
   332→                digits = match.group()
   333→                tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_LITERAL, f"{prefix}{digits}", line_num, col_pos))
   334→                pos += 2 + len(digits)
   335→                continue
   336→            else:
   337→                raise CircParseError(
   338→                    f"Binary/hex literal must have at least one digit",
   339→                    file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   340→                )
   341→
   342→        # Braces and brackets
   343→        if line[pos] in '{}[]':
   344→            tokens.append(Token(line[pos], line[pos], line_num, col_pos))
   345→            pos += 1
   346→            continue
   347→
   348→        # Colon
   349→        if line[pos] == ':':
   350→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COLON, ':', line_num, col_pos))
   351→            pos += 1
   352→            continue
   353→
   354→        # Comma
   355→        if line[pos] == ',':
   356→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_COMMA, ',', line_num, col_pos))
   357→            pos += 1
   358→            continue
   359→
   360→        # Equals sign
   361→        if line[pos] == '=':
   362→            tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EQ, '=', line_num, col_pos))
   363→            pos += 1
   364→            continue
   365→
   366→        # Unknown character
   367→        raise CircParseError(
   368→            f"Unexpected character: {line[pos]}",
   369→            file=None, line=line_num, col=col_pos
   370→        )
   371→
   372→    tokens.append(Token(TOKEN_EOF, '', line_num, len(line) + 1))
   373→    return tokens
   374→
   375→
   376→# =============================================================================
   377→# Parser
   378→# =============================================================================
   379→
   380→class Parser:
   381→    """Parser for circuit expressions."""
   382→
   383→    def __init__(self, tokens: List[Token], filepath: str):
   384→        self.tokens = tokens
   385→        self.filepath = filepath
   386→        self.pos = 0
   387→
   388→    def peek(self) -> Token:
   389→        return self.tokens[self.pos]
   390→
   391→    def consume(self, expected_type: Optional[str] = None,
   392→                expected_value: Optional[str] = None) -> Token:
   393→        tok = self.peek()
   394→        if expected_type is not None and tok.type != expected_type:
   395→            raise self.error(f"Expected token type {expected_type}, got {tok.type}")
   396→        if expected_value is not None and tok.value != expected_value:
   397→            raise self.error(f"Expected {expected_value}, got {tok.value}")
   398→        self.pos += 1
   399→        return tok
   400→
   401→    def error(self, message: str) -> CircParseError:
   402→        tok = self.peek()
   403→        return CircParseError(
   404→            message,
   405→            file=self.filepath,
   406→            line=tok.line,
   407→            col=tok.col
   408→        )
   409→
   410→    def parse_expression(self) -> ASTNode:
   411→        """Parse an expression: concatenation, signal ref, literal, or op call."""
   412→        tok = self.peek()
   413→
   414→        # If it's an identifier followed by [, it's a slice/index
   415→        if tok.type == TOKEN_ID:
   416→            name = tok.value
   417→            self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   418→
   419→            # Check for slice or index
   420→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   421→                return self.parse_signal_ref_with_select(name)
   422→            else:
   423→                return SignalRef(name)
   424→
   425→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LITERAL:
   426→            return self.parse_literal()
   427→
   428→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_LBRACE:
   429→            # Concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}
   430→            return self.parse_concat()
   431→
   432→        elif tok.type == TOKEN_OP:
   433→            # Operator call: OP(...)
   434→            return self.parse_op_call()
   435→
   436→        else:
   437→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token in expression: {tok.value}")
   438→
   439→    def parse_signal_ref_with_select(self, name: str) -> SignalRef:
   440→        """Parse a signal reference with optional slice/index: v[i] or v[hi:lo]"""
   441→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   442→
   443→        # Parse first number (msb or index)
   444→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   445→            raise self.error("Expected number in slice/index")
   446→        msb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   447→
   448→        # Check if it's a slice (with colon) or single bit index
   449→        if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   450→            self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)  # consume ':'
   451→
   452→            # Parse lsb
   453→            if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   454→                raise self.error("Expected number after colon in slice")
   455→            lsb_val = int(self.consume().value)
   456→
   457→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   458→
   459→            # Validate: msb >= lsb >= 0
   460→            if msb_val < lsb_val:
   461→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: msb ({msb_val}) < lsb ({lsb_val})")
   462→            if lsb_val < 0:
   463→                raise self.error(f"Invalid slice: lsb ({lsb_val}) < 0")
   464→
   465→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, lsb_val)
   466→        else:
   467→            # Single bit index
   468→            self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   469→            return SignalRef(name, msb_val, msb_val)  # msb=lsb for single bit
   470→
   471→    def parse_literal(self) -> Literal:
   472→        """Parse a literal value."""
   473→        tok = self.consume(TOKEN_LITERAL)
   474→        val = tok.value
   475→
   476→        if val in ('0', '1'):
   477→            return Literal(int(val), 1)
   478→        elif val.startswith('0b'):
   479→            # Binary literal: 0b1101
   480→            bits = val[2:]
   481→            if not bits:
   482→                raise self.error("Binary literal must have at least one digit")
   483→            value = int(bits, 2)
   484→            return Literal(value, len(bits))
   485→        elif val.startswith('0x'):
   486→            # Hex literal: 0x0f
   487→            hex_digits = val[2:]
   488→            if not hex_digits:
   489→                raise self.error("Hex literal must have at least one digit")
   490→            value = int(hex_digits, 16)
   491→            return Literal(value, len(hex_digits) * 4)
   492→        else:
   493→            # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
   494→            match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', val)
   495→            if match:
   496→                width = int(match.group(1))
   497→                radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
   498→                value_str = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   499→
   500→                try:
   501→                    if radix_str == 'b':
   502→                        value = int(value_str, 2)
   503→                    elif radix_str == 'h':
   504→                        value = int(value_str, 16)
   505→                    else:  # 'd'
   506→                        value = int(value_str, 10)
   507→                except ValueError:
   508→                    raise self.error(f"Invalid literal: {val}")
   509→
   510→                if value >= (1 << width):
   511→                    raise self.error(f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal")
   512→
   513→                return Literal(value, width)
   514→            else:
   515→                raise self.error(f"Invalid literal format: {val}")
   516→
   517→    def parse_concat(self) -> Concat:
   518→        """Parse concatenation: {e1, e2, ...}"""
   519→        self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACE)
   520→
   521→        parts = []
   522→        while True:
   523→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   524→                break
   525→
   526→            parts.append(self.parse_expression())
   527→
   528→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   529→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   530→                continue
   531→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RBRACE:
   532→                break
   533→
   534→        self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACE)
   535→        return Concat(parts)
   536→
   537→    def parse_op_call(self) -> OpCall:
   538→        """Parse operator call: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)"""
   539→        op_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_OP)
   540→        op = op_tok.value
   541→
   542→        self.consume(TOKEN_LPAREN)
   543→
   544→        args = []
   545→        while True:
   546→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   547→                break
   548→
   549→            args.append(self.parse_expression())
   550→
   551→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   552→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   553→                continue
   554→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_RPAREN:
   555→                break
   556→
   557→        self.consume(TOKEN_RPAREN)
   558→        return OpCall(op, args)
   559→
   560→    def parse_assignment_line(self) -> Tuple[str, ASTNode]:
   561→        """Parse an assignment line: lhs = expression"""
   562→        # Parse LHS (identifier)
   563→        lhs_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   564→        lhs = lhs_tok.value
   565→
   566→        self.consume(TOKEN_EQ)
   567→
   568→        # Parse RHS expression
   569→        expr = self.parse_expression()
   570→
   571→        # Make sure we're at EOF
   572→        if self.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
   573→            raise self.error(f"Unexpected token after expression: {self.peek().value}")
   574→
   575→        return lhs, expr
   576→
   577→    def parse_declaration(self) -> List[SignalDecl]:
   578→        """Parse a declaration line: INPUT/OUTPUT/WIRE name[msb:lsb]"""
   579→        kw_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_KW)
   580→        decl_type = kw_tok.value.lower()
   581→
   582→        decls = []
   583→        while True:
   584→            # Parse identifier
   585→            id_tok = self.consume(TOKEN_ID)
   586→            name = id_tok.value
   587→
   588→            # Check for width specifier
   589→            msb = None
   590→            lsb = None
   591→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_LBRACK:
   592→                self.consume(TOKEN_LBRACK)
   593→
   594→                # Parse msb
   595→                if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   596→                    raise self.error(f"Expected number after [ for {name}")
   597→                msb = int(self.consume().value)
   598→
   599→                # Check for slice or single bit
   600→                if self.peek().type == TOKEN_COLON:
   601→                    self.consume(TOKEN_COLON)
   602→
   603→                    if self.peek().type != TOKEN_NUMBER:
   604→                        raise self.error(f"Expected number after : for {name}")
   605→                    lsb = int(self.consume().value)
   606→
   607→                    if msb < lsb:
   608→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})")
   609→                    if lsb < 0:
   610→                        raise self.error(f"Invalid range for {name}: lsb ({lsb}) < 0")
   611→                else:
   612→                    # Single bit - msb=lsb
   613→                    lsb = msb
   614→
   615→                self.consume(TOKEN_RBRACK)
   616→
   617→            decls.append(SignalDecl(name, decl_type, msb, lsb))
   618→
   619→            # Check for more names or end of line
   620→            if self.peek().type == TOKEN_EOF:
   621→                break
   622→            elif self.peek().type == TOKEN_COMMA:
   623→                self.consume(TOKEN_COMMA)
   624→                continue
   625→            else:
   626→                raise self.error(f"Unexpected token: {self.peek().value}")
   627→
   628→        return decls
   629→
   630→
   631→def parse_circ_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
   632→    """Parse a .circ file and return (declarations, assignments).
   633→
   634→    Returns:
   635→        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
   636→        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples
   637→    """
   638→    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
   639→        lines = f.readlines()
   640→
   641→    declarations = []
   642→    assignments = []
   643→    seen_assignment = False
   644→
   645→    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
   646→        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')
   647→
   648→        # Skip empty lines and comments
   649→        stripped = line.lstrip()
   650→        if not stripped or stripped.startswith('#'):
   651→            continue
   652→
   653→        try:
   654→            tokens = tokenize(line, line_num)
   655→        except CircParseError as e:
   656→            if e.file is None:
   657→                e.file = filepath
   658→            raise
   659→
   660→        if not tokens:
   661→            continue
   662→
   663→        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
   664→
   665→        # Check if it's a declaration
   666→        if tokens[0].type == TOKEN_KW:
   667→            # Declaration line
   668→            if seen_assignment:
   669→                raise DeclarationAfterAssignmentError(
   670→                    f"Declaration found after assignment",
   671→                    file=filepath,
   672→                    line=line_num,
   673→                    col=1
   674→                )
   675→
   676→            try:
   677→                decls = parser.parse_declaration()
   678→                declarations.extend(decls)
   679→            except CircParseError as e:
   680→                if e.file is None:
   681→                    e.file = filepath
   682→                raise
   683→
   684→        elif tokens[0].type == TOKEN_ID:
   685→            # Assignment line
   686→            seen_assignment = True
   687→
   688→            try:
   689→                lhs, expr = parser.parse_assignment_line()
   690→                assignments.append((lhs, expr))
   691→            except CircParseError as e:
   692→                if e.file is None:
   693→                    e.file = filepath
   694→                raise
   695→        else:
   696→            raise CircParseError(
   697→                f"Expected keyword (input/output/wire) or identifier",
   698→                file=filepath,
   699→                line=line_num,
   700→                col=1
   701→            )
   702→
   703→    return declarations, assignments
   704→
   705→
   706→# =============================================================================
   707→# Expression Evaluator (for circuit evaluation)
   708→# =============================================================================
   709→
   710→def eval_expression(expr: ASTNode, signal_values: Dict[str, int],
   711→                    signal_widths: Dict[str, int],
   712→                    filepath: str) -> Tuple[int, int]:
   713→    """Evaluate an expression and return (value, width).
   714→
   715→    Args:
   716→        expr: AST node to evaluate
   717→        signal_values: Dict mapping signal names to their integer values
   718→        signal_widths: Dict mapping signal names to their widths
   719→        filepath: For error reporting
   720→
   721→    Returns:
   722→        Tuple of (value, width)
   723→    """
   724→    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
   725→        if expr.name in signal_values:
   726→            # Get the full signal value
   727→            full_value = signal_values[expr.name]
   728→            width = signal_widths[expr.name]
   729→
   730→            if expr.msb is None:
   731→                # Scalar reference
   732→                return full_value, 1
   733→            else:
   734→                # Slice or single bit
   735→                if expr.msb >= width or expr.lsb >= width:
   736→                    raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
   737→                        f"Index {expr.msb}:{expr.lsb} out of bounds for signal {expr.name} (width {width})",
   738→                        file=filepath
   739→                    )
   740→                # Extract bits from lsb to msb
   741→                mask = ((1 << (expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1)) - 1) << expr.lsb
   742→                slice_value = (full_value & mask) >> expr.lsb
   743→                return slice_value, expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1
   744→        else:
   745→            raise UndefinedNameError(
   746→                f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}",
   747→                file=filepath
   748→            )
   749→
   750→    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
   751→        return expr.value, expr.width
   752→
   753→    elif isinstance(expr, Concat):
   754→        # Evaluate all parts
   755→        parts_values = []
   756→        total_width = 0
   757→        for part in expr.parts:
   758→            val, width = eval_expression(part, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
   759→            parts_values.append((val, width))
   760→            total_width += width
   761→
   762→        # Concatenate: first part is MSB
   763→        result = 0
   764→        for val, width in parts_values:
   765→            result = (result << width) | val
   766→
   767→        return result, total_width
   768→
   769→    elif isinstance(expr, OpCall):
   770→        op = expr.op.upper()
   771→        args_results = []
   772→        for arg in expr.args:
   773→            val, width = eval_expression(arg, signal_values, signal_widths, filepath)
   774→            args_results.append((val, width))
   775→
   776→        # Bitwise operators (width-preserving)
   777→        if op in ('NOT', 'BUF'):
   778→            if len(args_results) != 1:
   779→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   780→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
   781→                    file=filepath
   782→                )
   783→            val, width = args_results[0]
   784→            if op == 'NOT':
   785→                return (~val) & ((1 << width) - 1), width
   786→            else:  # BUF
   787→                return val, width
   788→
   789→        elif op in ('AND', 'OR', 'XOR', 'NAND', 'NOR', 'XNOR'):
   790→            if len(args_results) < 2:
   791→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   792→                    f"Operator {op} requires at least 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
   793→                    file=filepath
   794→                )
   795→
   796→            # Check all operands have same width
   797→            first_width = args_results[0][1]
   798→            for val, width in args_results:
   799→                if width != first_width:
   800→                    raise WidthMismatchError(
   801→                        f"Operator {op}: operand widths {width} and {first_width} don't match",
   802→                        file=filepath
   803→                    )
   804→
   805→            mask = (1 << first_width) - 1
   806→            result = args_results[0][0]
   807→
   808→            if op == 'AND':
   809→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
   810→                    result &= val
   811→            elif op == 'OR':
   812→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
   813→                    result |= val
   814→            elif op == 'XOR':
   815→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
   816→                    result ^= val
   817→            elif op == 'NAND':
   818→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
   819→                    result &= val
   820→                result = mask & ~result
   821→            elif op == 'NOR':
   822→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
   823→                    result |= val
   824→                result = mask & ~result
   825→            elif op == 'XNOR':
   826→                for val, _ in args_results[1:]:
   827→                    result ^= val
   828→                result = mask & ~result
   829→
   830→            return result & mask, first_width
   831→
   832→        elif op in ('MUX', 'ITE'):
   833→            if len(args_results) != 3:
   834→                raise ArityError(
   835→                    f"Operator {op} requires 3 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
   836→                    file=filepath
   837→                )
   838→
   839→            sel_val, sel_width = args_results[0]
   840→            a_val, a_width = args_results[1]
   841→            b_val, b_width = args_results[2]
   842→
   843→            if sel_width != 1:
   844→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   845→                    f"Operator {op}: selector width {sel_width} must be 1",
   846→                    file=filepath
   847→                )
   848→            if a_width != b_width:
   849→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   850→                    f"Operator {op}: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
   851→                    file=filepath
   852→                )
   853→
   854→            result = a_val if sel_val else b_val
   855→            return result, a_width
   856→
   857→        elif op in ('REDUCE_AND', 'REDUCE_OR', 'REDUCE_XOR'):
   858→            if len(args_results) != 1:
   859→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   860→                    f"Operator {op} requires 1 operand, got {len(args_results)}",
   861→                    file=filepath
   862→                )
   863→
   864→            val, width = args_results[0]
   865→            if width < 1:
   866→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   867→                    f"Operator {op}: operand width {width} must be >= 1",
   868→                    file=filepath
   869→                )
   870→
   871→            mask = (1 << width) - 1
   872→            val &= mask
   873→
   874→            if op == 'REDUCE_AND':
   875→                # Check if all bits are 1
   876→                result = 1 if val == mask else 0
   877→            elif op == 'REDUCE_OR':
   878→                result = 1 if val != 0 else 0
   879→            else:  # REDUCE_XOR
   880→                result = 0
   881→                for i in range(width):
   882→                    result ^= (val >> i) & 1
   883→
   884→            return result, 1
   885→
   886→        elif op == 'EQ':
   887→            if len(args_results) != 2:
   888→                raise ArityError(
   889→                    f"Operator EQ requires 2 operands, got {len(args_results)}",
   890→                    file=filepath
   891→                )
   892→
   893→            a_val, a_width = args_results[0]
   894→            b_val, b_width = args_results[1]
   895→
   896→            if a_width != b_width:
   897→                raise WidthMismatchError(
   898→                    f"Operator EQ: operand widths {a_width} and {b_width} don't match",
   899→                    file=filepath
   900→                )
   901→
   902→            return int(a_val == b_val), 1
   903→
   904→        else:
   905→            raise ArityError(
   906→                f"Unknown operator: {op}",
   907→                file=filepath
   908→            )
   909→
   910→    else:
   911→        raise ValueError(f"Unknown AST node type: {type(expr)}")
   912→
   913→
   914→def parse_input_literal(value_str: str, expected_width: int,
   915→                        filepath: str, line: int = None, col: int = None) -> int:
   916→    """Parse an input literal value and return the integer value.
   917→
   918→    Validates that the width matches the expected width.
   919→    """
   920→    if value_str in ('0', '1'):
   921→        if expected_width != 1:
   922→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
   923→                f"Input width mismatch: got 1 but expected {expected_width}",
   924→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   925→            )
   926→        return int(value_str)
   927→
   928→    elif value_str.startswith('0b'):
   929→        bits = value_str[2:]
   930→        if not bits:
   931→            raise InputValueParseError(
   932→                f"Binary literal must have at least one digit",
   933→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   934→            )
   935→        value = int(bits, 2)
   936→        width = len(bits)
   937→
   938→        if width != expected_width:
   939→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
   940→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
   941→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   942→            )
   943→        return value
   944→
   945→    elif value_str.startswith('0x'):
   946→        hex_digits = value_str[2:]
   947→        if not hex_digits:
   948→            raise InputValueParseError(
   949→                f"Hex literal must have at least one digit",
   950→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   951→            )
   952→        value = int(hex_digits, 16)
   953→        width = len(hex_digits) * 4
   954→
   955→        if width != expected_width:
   956→            raise InputWidthMismatchError(
   957→                f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
   958→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   959→            )
   960→        return value
   961→
   962→    else:
   963→        # Sized literal: 8'b1010, 8'hff, 8'd200
   964→        match = re.match(r'(\d+)\'[bBhHdD]([\d01a-fA-F_]+)', value_str)
   965→        if match:
   966→            width = int(match.group(1))
   967→            radix_str = match.group(2).lower()
   968→            value_str_clean = match.group(3).replace('_', '')
   969→
   970→            if width != expected_width:
   971→                raise InputWidthMismatchError(
   972→                    f"Input width mismatch: got {width} but expected {expected_width}",
   973→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   974→                )
   975→
   976→            try:
   977→                if radix_str == 'b':
   978→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 2)
   979→                elif radix_str == 'h':
   980→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 16)
   981→                else:  # 'd'
   982→                    value = int(value_str_clean, 10)
   983→            except ValueError:
   984→                raise InputValueParseError(
   985→                    f"Invalid literal: {value_str}",
   986→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   987→                )
   988→
   989→            if value >= (1 << width):
   990→                raise CircParseError(
   991→                    f"Value {value} too large for {width}-bit literal",
   992→                    file=filepath, line=line, col=col
   993→                )
   994→
   995→            return value
   996→        else:
   997→            raise InputValueParseError(
   998→                f"Invalid input literal format: {value_str}",
   999→                file=filepath, line=line, col=col
  1000→            )
  1001→
  1002→
  1003→def format_vector_value(value: int, width: int, radix: str) -> str:
  1004→    """Format a vector value according to the specified radix.
  1005→
  1006→    Args:
  1007→        value: Integer value
  1008→        width: Bit width
  1009→        radix: 'bin', 'hex', or 'dec'
  1010→
  1011→    Returns:
  1012→        Formatted string
  1013→    """
  1014→    value &= (1 << width) - 1
  1015→
  1016→    if radix == 'bin':
  1017→        return f"0b{value:0{width}b}"
  1018→    elif radix == 'hex':
  1019→        # Calculate minimum number of hex digits
  1020→        hex_digits = (width + 3) // 4
  1021→        return f"0x{value:0{hex_digits}x}"
  1022→    elif radix == 'dec':
  1023→        return str(value)
  1024→

  1026→# =============================================================================
  1027→# Validation
  1028→# =============================================================================
  1029→
  1030→def validate_circ_file(filepath: str, declarations: List[SignalDecl],
  1031→                       assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]) -> Dict[str, object]:
  1032→    """Validate declarations and assignments, compute widths."""
  1033→
  1034→    # Track all declared signals
  1035→    inputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1036→    outputs: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1037→    wires: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1038→    all_signals: Dict[str, SignalDecl] = {}
  1039→
  1040→    # Process declarations
  1041→    for decl in declarations:
  1042→        if decl.name in all_signals:
  1043→            raise DuplicateNameError(
  1044→                f"Duplicate signal name: {decl.name}",
  1045→                file=filepath
  1046→            )
  1047→        all_signals[decl.name] = decl
  1048→        if decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1049→            inputs[decl.name] = decl
  1050→        elif decl.sig_type == 'output':
  1051→            outputs[decl.name] = decl
  1052→        elif decl.sig_type == 'wire':
  1053→            wires[decl.name] = decl
  1054→
  1055→    # Track assignments
  1056→    assigned: Dict[str, Tuple[str, int]] = {}  # name -> (op, line_num)
  1057→
  1058→    # Build dependency graph for cycle detection
  1059→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1060→
  1061→    # Operator arities
  1062→    op_arity = {
  1063→        'NOT': 1,
  1064→        'BUF': 1,
  1065→        'AND': 2,
  1066→        'OR': 2,
  1067→        'XOR': 2,
  1068→        'NAND': 2,
  1069→        'NOR': 2,
  1070→        'XNOR': 2,
  1071→        'MUX': 3,
  1072→        'ITE': 3,
  1073→        'REDUCE_AND': 1,
  1074→        'REDUCE_OR': 1,
  1075→        'REDUCE_XOR': 1,
  1076→        'EQ': 2,
  1077→    }
  1078→
  1079→    for i, (lhs, expr) in enumerate(assignments):
  1080→        line_num = i + 1
  1081→
  1082→        # Check LHS is valid
  1083→        if lhs not in all_signals:
  1084→            raise UndefinedNameError(
  1085→                f"Undefined signal: {lhs}",
  1086→                file=filepath
  1087→            )
  1088→
  1089→        signal_decl = all_signals[lhs]
  1090→        if signal_decl.sig_type == 'input':
  1091→            raise InputAssignmentError(
  1092→                f"Cannot assign to input: {lhs}",
  1093→                file=filepath
  1094→            )
  1095→
  1096→        # Check for multiple assignment
  1097→        if lhs in assigned:
  1098→            raise MultipleAssignmentError(
  1099→                f"Multiple assignment to: {lhs}",
  1100→                file=filepath
  1101→            )
  1102→
  1103→        assigned[lhs] = ('expr', line_num)
  1104→
  1105→        # Check expression and collect dependencies
  1106→        graph[lhs] = []
  1107→
  1108→        def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1109→            if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1110→                if node.msb is not None:  # Has slice
  1111→                    # Check bounds against declared width if available
  1112→                    if node.name in all_signals:
  1113→                        decl = all_signals[node.name]
  1114→                        width = decl.width
  1115→                        if node.msb >= width or node.lsb >= width:
  1116→                            raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
  1117→                                f"Index {node.msb}:{node.lsb} out of bounds for signal {node.name} (width {width})",
  1118→                                file=filepath
  1119→                            )
  1120→                if node.name not in ('0', '1'):
  1121→                    graph[lhs].append(node.name)
  1122→            elif isinstance(node, Literal):
  1123→                pass  # literals have no deps
  1124→            elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1125→                for part in node.parts:
  1126→                    collect_deps(part)
  1127→            elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1128→                # Check arity
  1129→                op_upper = node.op.upper()
  1130→                if op_upper in op_arity:
  1131→                    min_arity = op_arity[op_upper]
  1132→                    if len(node.args) < min_arity:
  1133→                        raise ArityError(
  1134→                            f"Operator {node.op} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(node.args)}",
  1135→                            file=filepath
  1136→                        )
  1137→                else:
  1138→                    raise ArityError(
  1139→                        f"Unknown operator: {node.op}",
  1140→                        file=filepath
  1141→                    )
  1142→                for arg in node.args:
  1143→                    collect_deps(arg)
  1144→
  1145→        try:
  1146→            collect_deps(expr)
  1147→        except (IndexOutOfBoundsError, ArityError):
  1148→            raise
  1149→        except Exception as e:
  1150→            raise CircParseError(
  1151→                f"Error in expression: {e}",
  1152→                file=filepath
  1153→            )
  1154→
  1155→    # Check for unassigned signals (only wires and outputs)
  1156→    for name, sig_type in [('wire', wires), ('output', outputs)]:
  1157→        for decl in all_signals.values():
  1158→            if decl.sig_type in ('wire', 'output'):
  1159→                if decl.name not in assigned:
  1160→                    raise UnassignedSignalError(
  1161→                        f"Unassigned signal: {decl.name}",
  1162→                        file=filepath
  1163→                    )
  1164→
  1165→    # Check for cycles
  1166→    def find_cycle() -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1167→        visited: Set[str] = set()
  1168→        rec_stack: List[str] = []
  1169→
  1170→        def dfs(node: str) -> Optional[List[str]]:
  1171→            visited.add(node)
  1172→            rec_stack.append(node)
  1173→
  1174→            for neighbor in graph.get(node, []):
  1175→                if neighbor not in visited:
  1176→                    result = dfs(neighbor)
  1177→                    if result:
  1178→                        return result
  1179→                elif neighbor in rec_stack:
  1180→                    start_idx = rec_stack.index(neighbor)
  1181→                    return rec_stack[start_idx:] + [node]
  1182→
  1183→            rec_stack.pop()
  1184→            return None
  1185→
  1186→        for node in graph:
  1187→            if node not in visited:
  1188→                cycle = dfs(node)
  1189→                if cycle:
  1190→                    return cycle
  1191→        return None
  1192→
  1193→    cycle = find_cycle()
  1194→    if cycle:
  1195→        cycle_path = " -> ".join(cycle)
  1196→        raise CycleError(
  1197→            f"Cycle detected: {cycle_path}",
  1198→            file=filepath
  1199→        )
  1200→
  1201→    # Return success info
  1202→    sorted_inputs = sorted(inputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1203→    sorted_outputs = sorted(outputs.values(), key=lambda d: d.name)
  1204→
  1205→    return {
  1206→        'inputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_inputs],
  1207→        'outputs': [{'name': d.name, 'msb': d.msb, 'lsb': d.lsb} for d in sorted_outputs],
  1208→    }
  1209→

  1242→def cmd_check(filepath: str, json_output: bool) -> int:
  1243→    """Execute check command."""
  1244→    # Check file exists first
  1245→    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
  1246→        if json_output:
  1247→            print(format_error_json(
  1248→                FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {filepath}", filepath),
  1249→                'check'
  1250→            ))
  1251→        else:
  1252→            print(f"Error: File not found: {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)
  1253→        return 1
  1254→
  1255→    try:
  1256→        declarations, assignments = parse_circ_file(filepath)
  1257→        result = validate_circ_file(filepath, declarations, assignments)
  1258→
  1259→        if json_output:
  1260→            output = {
  1261→                'ok': True,
  1262→                'command': 'check',
  1263→                'format': 'circ',
  1264→                'inputs': result['inputs'],
  1265→                'outputs': result['outputs'],
  1266→            }
  1267→            print(json.dumps(output))
  1268→        else:
  1269→            input_strs = []
  1270→            for i in result['inputs']:
  1271→                if i['msb'] is not None:
  1272→                    input_strs.append(f"{i['name']}[{i['msb']}:{i['lsb']}]")
  1273→                else:
  1274→                    input_strs.append(i['name'])
  1275→            output_strs = []
  1276→            for o in result['outputs']:
  1277→                if o['msb'] is not None:
  1278→                    output_strs.append(f"{o['name']}[{o['msb']}:{o['lsb']}]")
  1279→                else:
  1280→                    output_strs.append(o['name'])
  1281→            print(f"Inputs: {', '.join(input_strs)}")
  1282→            print(f"Outputs: {', '.join(output_strs)}")
  1283→
  1284→        return 0
  1285→
  1286→    except CircuitError as e:
  1287→        if json_output:
  1288→            print(format_error_json(e, 'check'))
  1289→        else:
  1290→            print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1291→        return e.exit_code

  1294→# =============================================================================
  1295→# 2-Valued Evaluation
  1296→# =============================================================================
  1297→
  1298→def cmd_eval(filepath: str, set_inputs: List[str], default: Optional[int],
  1299→             allow_extra: bool, json_output: bool, radix: str, mode: str) -> int:
  1300→    """Execute eval command with vector support."""
  1301→
  1302→    # Check file exists
  1303→    if not os.path.exists(filepath):
  1304→        if json_output:
  1305→            print(json.dumps({
  1306→                'ok': False,
  1307→                'command': 'eval',
  1308→                'exit_code': 1,
  1309→                'error': {
  1310→                    'type': 'FileNotFoundError',
  1311→                    'message': f"File not found: {filepath}",
  1312→                    'file': filepath,
  1313→                    'line': None,
  1314→                    'col': None,
  1315→                }
  1316→            }))
  1317→        else:
  1318→            print(f"Error: File not found: {filepath}", file=sys.stderr)
  1319→        return 1
  1320→
  1321→    try:
  1322→        declarations, assignments = parse_circ_file(filepath)
  1323→        result_info = validate_circ_file(filepath, declarations, assignments)
  1324→    except CircuitError as e:
  1325→        if json_output:
  1326→            print(format_error_json(e, 'eval'))
  1327→        else:
  1328→            print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1329→        return e.exit_code
  1330→
  1331→    # Check radix restriction for 3val mode
  1332→    if mode == '3val' and radix != 'bin':
  1333→        error = RadixNotAllowedIn3ValError(
  1334→            f"Radix {radix} not allowed in 3-valued mode",
  1335→            file=filepath
  1336→        )
  1337→        if json_output:
  1338→            print(format_error_json(error, 'eval'))
  1339→        else:
  1340→            print(f"Error: {error.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1341→        return error.exit_code
  1342→
  1343→    # Build signal width lookup
  1344→    signal_widths = {}
  1345→    for decl in declarations:
  1346→        signal_widths[decl.name] = decl.width
  1347→
  1348→    # Parse input assignments
  1349→    input_values = {}
  1350→    for assignment in set_inputs:
  1351→        if '=' not in assignment:
  1352→            if json_output:
  1353→                print(json.dumps({
  1354→                    'ok': False,
  1355→                    'command': 'eval',
  1356→                    'exit_code': 2,
  1357→                    'error': {
  1358→                        'type': 'InputValueParseError',
  1359→                        'message': f"Invalid input format: {assignment}. Expected name=value",
  1360→                        'file': None,
  1361→                        'line': None,
  1362→                        'col': None,
  1363→                    }
  1364→                }))
  1365→            else:
  1366→                print(f"Error: Invalid input format: {assignment}. Expected name=value", file=sys.stderr)
  1367→            return 2
  1368→
  1369→        name, value_str = assignment.split('=', 1)
  1370→        name = name.strip()
  1371→        value_str = value_str.strip()
  1372→
  1373→        # Get expected width for this input
  1374→        expected_width = signal_widths.get(name)
  1375→        if expected_width is None:
  1376→            if not allow_extra:
  1377→                if json_output:
  1378→                    print(json.dumps({
  1379→                        'ok': False,
  1380→                        'command': 'eval',
  1381→                        'exit_code': 1,
  1382→                        'error': {
  1383→                            'type': 'UnknownInputError',
  1384→                            'message': f"Unknown input: {name}",
  1385→                            'file': None,
  1386→                            'line': None,
  1387→                            'col': None,
  1388→                        }
  1389→                    }))
  1390→                else:
  1391→                    print(f"Error: Unknown input: {name}", file=sys.stderr)
  1392→                return 1
  1393→            # Allow extra inputs if --allow-extra is set
  1394→            # We'll just store as-is with width 1 for now
  1395→            # but we need width for evaluation
  1396→            input_values[name] = int(value_str)  # Just try to parse as int for simplicity
  1397→            continue
  1398→
  1399→        try:
  1400→            value = parse_input_literal(value_str, expected_width, filepath)
  1401→            input_values[name] = value
  1402→        except InputWidthMismatchError as e:
  1403→            if json_output:
  1404→                print(format_error_json(e, 'eval'))
  1405→            else:
  1406→                print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1407→            return e.exit_code
  1408→        except InputValueParseError as e:
  1409→            if json_output:
  1410→                print(format_error_json(e, 'eval'))
  1411→            else:
  1412→                print(f"Error: {e.message}", file=sys.stderr)
  1413→            return e.exit_code
  1414→
  1415→    # Get declared input names
  1416→    declared_inputs = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'input'}
  1417→
  1418→    # Check for missing inputs
  1419→    missing_inputs = declared_inputs - set(input_values.keys())
  1420→    if missing_inputs:
  1421→        if default is None:
  1422→            if json_output:
  1423→                print(json.dumps({
  1424→                    'ok': False,
  1425→                    'command': 'eval',
  1426→                    'exit_code': 1,
  1427→                    'error': {
  1428→                        'type': 'MissingInputError',
  1429→                        'message': f"Missing inputs: {', '.join(sorted(missing_inputs))}",
  1430→                        'file': None,
  1431→                        'line': None,
  1432→                        'col': None,
  1433→                    }
  1434→                }))
  1435→            else:
  1436→                print(f"Error: Missing inputs: {', '.join(sorted(missing_inputs))}", file=sys.stderr)
  1437→            return 1
  1438→        else:
  1439→            # Fill missing inputs with default value
  1440→            for name in missing_inputs:
  1441→                width = signal_widths[name]
  1442→                # Fill with all bits set to default
  1443→                input_values[name] = default * ((1 << width) - 1)
  1444→
  1445→    # Build dependency graph for topological evaluation
  1446→    graph: Dict[str, List[str]] = {}
  1447→    for lhs, expr in assignments:
  1448→        graph[lhs] = []
  1449→        # Collect dependencies
  1450→        def collect_deps(node: ASTNode):
  1451→            if isinstance(node, SignalRef):
  1452→                if node.name not in ('0', '1'):
  1453→                    graph[lhs].append(node.name)
  1454→            elif isinstance(node, Concat):
  1455→                for part in node.parts:
  1456→                    collect_deps(part)
  1457→            elif isinstance(node, OpCall):
  1458→                for arg in node.args:
  1459→                    collect_deps(arg)
  1460→
  1461→        collect_deps(expr)
  1462→
  1463→    # Evaluate in topological order
  1464→    # Process assignments repeatedly until all are computed
  1465→    values = dict(input_values)  # Start with input values
  1466→
  1467→    max_iterations = len(assignments) * 10  # Safety limit
  1468→    for _ in range(max_iterations):
  1469→        progress = False
  1470→        for lhs, expr in assignments:
  1471→            if lhs in values:
  1472→                continue  # Already computed
  1473→
  1474→            if all(dep in values for dep in graph[lhs]):
  1475→                val, width = eval_expression(expr, values, signal_widths, filepath)
  1476→                values[lhs] = val
  1477→                progress = True
  1478→
  1479→        if not progress:
  1480→            break
  1481→
  1482→    # Check for uncomputed values (cycles or missing dependencies)
  1483→    for lhs, _ in assignments:
  1484→        if lhs not in values:
  1485→            # Find what's missing
  1486→            for dep in graph[lhs]:
  1487→                if dep not in values:
  1488→                    raise CycleError(
  1489→                        f"Cannot evaluate: missing dependency {dep} for {lhs}",
  1490→                        file=filepath
  1491→                    )
  1492→
  1493→    # Get output values
  1494→    outputs = []
  1495→    for out_info in result_info['outputs']:
  1496→        out_name = out_info['name']
  1497→        width = signal_widths[out_name]
  1498→        value = values.get(out_name, 0)
  1499→
  1500→        if width == 1:
  1501→            # Scalar output: always show 0 or 1
  1502→            formatted = str(value & 1)
  1503→        else:
  1504→            # Vector output: use specified radix
  1505→            formatted = format_vector_value(value, width, radix)
  1506→
  1507→        outputs.append({
  1508→            'name': out_name,
  1509→            'msb': out_info['msb'],
  1510→            'lsb': out_info['lsb'],
  1511→            'value': formatted
  1512→        })
  1513→
  1514→    # Sort outputs by name
  1515→    outputs.sort(key=lambda x: x['name'])
  1516→
  1517→    # Output results
  1518→    if json_output:
  1519→        print(json.dumps({
  1520→            'ok': True,
  1521→            'command': 'eval',
  1522→            'mode': '2val',
  1523→            'radix': radix,
  1524→            'outputs': outputs
  1525→        }))
  1526→    else:
  1527→        for out in outputs:
  1528→            print(f"{out['name']}={out['value']}")
  1529→
  1530→    return 0

  1549→def main():
  1550→    import argparse

        signal_widths: Dict mapping signal names to their widths
        filepath: For error reporting

    Returns:
        Tuple of (value, width)

    if isinstance(expr, SignalRef):
        if expr.name in signal_values:
            # Get the full signal value
            full_value = signal_values[expr.name]

            if expr.msb is None:
                # Scalar reference
                return full_value, 1
            else:
                # Slice or single bit
                if expr.msb >= width or expr.lsb >= width:
                    raise IndexOutOfBoundsError(
                        f"Index {expr.msb}:{expr.lsb} out of bounds for signal {expr.name} (width {width})",
                        file=filepath
                    )
                # Extract bits from lsb to msb
                mask = ((1 << (expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1)) - 1) << expr.lsb
                slice_value = (full_value & mask) >> expr.lsb
                return slice_value, expr.msb - expr.lsb + 1
        else:
            raise UndefinedNameError(
                f"Undefined signal: {expr.name}",
                file=filepath
            )

    elif isinstance(expr, Literal):
        return expr.value, expr.width

# =============================================================================
# JSON Circuit Format Parser
# =============================================================================

def parse_json_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
    """Parse a JSON circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).

    Returns:
        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects
        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples

    Raises:
        JsonParseError: Invalid JSON syntax
        JsonSchemaError: JSON doesn't match expected schema
        CircParseError: Expression parsing error
    """
    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
        try:
            data = json.load(f)
        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
            raise JsonParseError(
                f"Invalid JSON syntax: {e}",
                file=filepath,
                line=e.lineno,
                col=e.colno
            )

    # Validate schema
    if not isinstance(data, dict):
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            "JSON root must be an object",
            file=filepath
        )

    required_keys = {'format_version', 'inputs', 'outputs', 'wires', 'assignments'}
    missing = required_keys - set(data.keys())
    if missing:
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            f"Missing required keys: {', '.join(sorted(missing))}",
            file=filepath
        )

    # Check format_version
    if data['format_version'] != 1:
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            f"Unsupported format_version: {data['format_version']}. Expected 1.",
            file=filepath
        )

    # Helper to validate port object
    def validate_port(port: dict, key_name: str) -> dict:
        if not isinstance(port, dict):
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"{key_name} must be an array of objects",
                file=filepath
            )
        if 'name' not in port:
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"Port object missing 'name' field",
                file=filepath
            )
        msb = port.get('msb')
        lsb = port.get('lsb')

        if msb is None and lsb is None:
            # Scalar port - valid
            return port

        if not isinstance(msb, int) or not isinstance(lsb, int):
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"Port '{port.get('name', '?')}': msb and lsb must be integers",
                file=filepath
            )

        if msb < lsb:
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"Port '{port['name']}': msb ({msb}) < lsb ({lsb})",
                file=filepath
            )

        if lsb < 0:
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"Port '{port['name']}': lsb ({lsb}) < 0",
                file=filepath
            )

        return port

    # Parse inputs
    inputs_list = data['inputs']
    if not isinstance(inputs_list, list):
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            "'inputs' must be an array",
            file=filepath
        )

    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
    seen_names: Set[str] = set()

    for i, port in enumerate(inputs_list):
        validate_port(port, f"inputs[{i}]")
        name = port['name']
        if name in seen_names:
            raise DuplicateNameError(
                f"Duplicate input name: {name}",
                file=filepath
            )
        seen_names.add(name)

        msb = port.get('msb')
        lsb = port.get('lsb')
        # Convert None/None to None, None for scalar; otherwise keep values
        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', msb_val, lsb_val))

    # Parse outputs
    outputs_list = data['outputs']
    if not isinstance(outputs_list, list):
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            "'outputs' must be an array",
            file=filepath
        )

    for i, port in enumerate(outputs_list):
        validate_port(port, f"outputs[{i}]")
        name = port['name']
        if name in seen_names:
            raise DuplicateNameError(
                f"Duplicate output name: {name}",
                file=filepath
            )
        seen_names.add(name)

        msb = port.get('msb')
        lsb = port.get('lsb')
        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'output', msb_val, lsb_val))

    # Parse wires
    wires_list = data['wires']
    if not isinstance(wires_list, list):
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            "'wires' must be an array",
            file=filepath
        )

    for i, port in enumerate(wires_list):
        validate_port(port, f"wires[{i}]")
        name = port['name']
        if name in seen_names:
            raise DuplicateNameError(
                f"Duplicate wire name: {name}",
                file=filepath
            )
        seen_names.add(name)

        msb = port.get('msb')
        lsb = port.get('lsb')
        msb_val = msb if msb is not None else None
        lsb_val = lsb if lsb is not None else None
        declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'wire', msb_val, lsb_val))

    # Parse assignments
    assignments_list = data['assignments']
    if not isinstance(assignments_list, list):
        raise JsonSchemaError(
            "'assignments' must be an array",
            file=filepath
        )

    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []
    lhs_seen: Set[str] = set()

    for i, assn in enumerate(assignments_list):
        if not isinstance(assn, dict):
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"assignments[{i}] must be an object",
                file=filepath
            )
        if 'lhs' not in assn:
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'lhs' field",
                file=filepath
            )
        if 'rhs' not in assn:
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"assignments[{i}] missing 'rhs' field",
                file=filepath
            )

        lhs = assn['lhs']
        rhs_str = assn['rhs']

        if not isinstance(lhs, str):
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"assignments[{i}].lhs must be a string",
                file=filepath
            )
        if not isinstance(rhs_str, str):
            raise JsonSchemaError(
                f"assignments[{i}].rhs must be a string",
                file=filepath
            )

        if lhs in lhs_seen:
            raise RedefinitionError(
                f"Signal '{lhs}' defined multiple times",
                file=filepath
            )
        lhs_seen.add(lhs)

        # Parse RHS expression using existing expression parser
        # Create a temporary tokens list by tokenizing the expression string
        # We'll reuse the existing tokenize function by creating a mock line
        try:
            tokens = tokenize(rhs_str, 1)  # line 1 for expression error context
        except CircParseError as e:
            # Adjust line/col to point to the assignment
            raise CircParseError(
                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
                file=filepath,
                line=None,
                col=None
            )

        parser = Parser(tokens, filepath)
        try:
            expr = parser.parse_expression()
        except CircParseError as e:
            raise CircParseError(
                f"Error in assignment {i}: {e.message}",
                file=filepath
            )

        # Ensure we consumed all tokens (no trailing junk)
        if parser.peek().type != TOKEN_EOF:
            raise CircParseError(
                f"Unexpected token after expression in assignment {i}",
                file=filepath
            )

        assignments.append((lhs, expr))

    # Validate that every wire and output appears exactly once as assignment LHS
    wire_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'wire'}
    output_names = {d.name for d in declarations if d.sig_type == 'output'}
    assigned_names = set(lhs_seen)

    unassigned = (wire_names | output_names) - assigned_names
    if unassigned:
        raise UnassignedSignalError(
            f"Unassigned signals: {', '.join(sorted(unassigned))}",
            file=filepath
        )

    # Check for extra assignments (LHS not declared)
    declared_names = {d.name for d in declarations}
    extra_assigned = assigned_names - declared_names
    if extra_assigned:
        raise UndefinedNameError(
            f"Assignment to undeclared signal(s): {', '.join(sorted(extra_assigned))}",
            file=filepath
        )

    return declarations, assignments


# =============================================================================
# BENCH Circuit Format Parser
# =============================================================================

def parse_bench_file(filepath: str) -> Tuple[List[SignalDecl], List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]]]:
    """Parse a BENCH format circuit file and return (declarations, assignments).

    BENCH is a scalar-only format commonly used in circuit benchmarks.

    Returns:
        declarations: List of SignalDecl objects (all scalar)
        assignments: List of (lhs, expression) tuples

    Raises:
        BenchParseError: Syntax error in .bench file
        RedefinitionError: Signal defined multiple times
        UndefinedNameError: Undefined name referenced
    """
    with open(filepath, 'r') as f:
        lines = f.readlines()

    declarations: List[SignalDecl] = []
    assignments: List[Tuple[str, ASTNode]] = []

    # Track all signals (inputs, wires, outputs)
    signal_names: Set[str] = set()
    input_names: Set[str] = set()
    output_names: Set[str] = set()
    wire_names: Set[str] = set()

    # Track assigned signals (for RedefinitionError)
    assigned_lhs: Set[str] = set()

    # For expression parsing
    op_arity = {
        'NOT': 1,
        'BUF': 1,
        'AND': 2,
        'OR': 2,
        'XOR': 2,
        'NAND': 2,
        'NOR': 2,
        'XNOR': 2,
    }

    for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
        line = line.rstrip('\n\r')

        # Strip comment
        if '#' in line:
            line = line[:line.index('#')]

        # Skip empty lines
        stripped = line.strip()
        if not stripped:
            continue

        # Remove all whitespace for easier parsing
        no_whitespace = ''.join(stripped.split())

        # Check for INPUT(name)
        if no_whitespace.startswith('INPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
            name = no_whitespace[6:-1]  # Remove 'INPUT(' and ')'
            if not name:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Empty input name at line {line_num}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            # Check for brackets (vector support not allowed in BENCH)
            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            if name in signal_names:
                raise RedefinitionError(
                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            signal_names.add(name)
            input_names.add(name)
            declarations.append(SignalDecl(name, 'input', None, None))
            continue

        # Check for OUTPUT(name)
        if no_whitespace.startswith('OUTPUT(') and no_whitespace.endswith(')'):
            name = no_whitespace[7:-1]  # Remove 'OUTPUT(' and ')'
            if not name:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Empty output name at line {line_num}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            if '[' in name or ']' in name:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {name}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            if name in signal_names:
                raise RedefinitionError(
                    f"Duplicate signal definition: {name}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            signal_names.add(name)
            output_names.add(name)
            # OUTPUT is just a reference, no declaration as wire
            # It will be validated later against assignments
            continue

        # Check for assignment: lhs = OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
        if '=' in no_whitespace:
            parts = no_whitespace.split('=', 1)
            if len(parts) != 2:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Invalid assignment at line {line_num}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            lhs = parts[0]
            rhs = parts[1]

            # Validate LHS
            if not lhs:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Empty LHS in assignment at line {line_num}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            if '[' in lhs or ']' in lhs:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {lhs}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            # Check for redefinition
            if lhs in assigned_lhs:
                raise RedefinitionError(
                    f"Redefinition of signal: {lhs}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            assigned_lhs.add(lhs)

            # Add as wire if not already declared as input/output
            if lhs not in signal_names:
                signal_names.add(lhs)
                wire_names.add(lhs)
                declarations.append(SignalDecl(lhs, 'wire', None, None))

            # Parse RHS: OP(arg1, arg2, ...)
            # Find operator name
            op_match = None
            for op in op_arity:
                if rhs.startswith(op + '(') and rhs.endswith(')'):
                    op_match = op
                    break

            if op_match is None:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Unknown or malformed operator call: {rhs}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            args_str = rhs[len(op_match)+1:-1]  # Remove OP( and )
            if args_str:
                arg_names = [arg.strip() for arg in args_str.split(',')]
            else:
                arg_names = []

            # Validate argument count
            min_arity = op_arity[op_match]
            if len(arg_names) < min_arity:
                raise BenchParseError(
                    f"Operator {op_match} requires at least {min_arity} operands, got {len(arg_names)}",
                    file=filepath,
                    line=line_num,
                    col=1
                )

            # Check for literals (BENCH doesn't allow literals in RHS)
            for arg in arg_names:
                # Literals: 0, 1, 0b..., 0x..., sized literals
                if arg in ('0', '1'):
                    raise BenchParseError(
                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
                        file=filepath,
                        line=line_num,
                        col=1
                    )
                if arg.startswith('0b') or arg.startswith('0x'):
                    raise BenchParseError(
                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
                        file=filepath,
                        line=line_num,
                        col=1
                    )
                if re.match(r'^\d+[\'[bBhHdD]', arg):
                    raise BenchParseError(
                        f"Literals not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
                        file=filepath,
                        line=line_num,
                        col=1
                    )

                # Check for brackets (vector not allowed)
                if '[' in arg or ']' in arg:
                    raise BenchParseError(
                        f"Vector notation not allowed in BENCH: {arg}",
                        file=filepath,
                        line=line_num,
                        col=1
                    )

            # Build AST: OpCall with SignalRef arguments
            args_ast = []
            for arg in arg_names:
                # BENCH is scalar-only, no indices/slices
                args_ast.append(SignalRef(arg))

            expr = OpCall(op_match, args_ast)
            assignments.append((lhs, expr))
            continue

        # If we get here, the line is invalid
        raise BenchParseError(
            f"Invalid statement: {stripped}",
            file=filepath,
            line=line_num,
            col=1
        )

    # Validate OUTPUT references
    for out_name in output_names:
        if out_name not in signal_names:
            raise UndefinedNameError(
                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
                file=filepath
            )
        # Also check that OUTPUT is assigned somewhere (either as input or assigned)
        if out_name not in assigned_lhs and out_name not in input_names:
            raise UndefinedNameError(
                f"OUTPUT references undefined signal: {out_name}",
                file=filepath
            )

    # Validate that all assigned signals (except inputs) are defined
    for lhs in assigned_lhs:
        if lhs not in input_names and lhs not in wire_names:
            # Already added as wire, but check consistency
            pass

    # Also: wires must be assigned (already tracked via assigned_lhs)
    for wname in wire_names:
        if wname not in assigned_lhs:
            raise UnassignedSignalError(
                f"Unassigned wire: {wname}",
                file=filepath
            )

    return declarations, assignments
